tr.th: Conclusion and Recommendations based on this report and previous findings known to us

tr.td: The patient shows elevated serum creatinine, low CO₂, high chloride, and significant proteinuria, glycosuria, and ketonuria, with low urine specific gravity and abnormal urine appearance. These findings suggest impaired renal function, possible tubular dysfunction, and metabolic derangement. Low HbA1c argues against chronic hyperglycemia. Recommend urgent nephrology evaluation to assess for acute or chronic kidney disease and further investigate the etiology of renal and metabolic abnormalities.

display.value: Proteinuria due to type 2 diabetes mellitus
Microalbuminuria due to type 2 diabetes mellitus
Disorder of kidney due to diabetes mellitus
